below Sitamau. The city of Ujjain is situated on its bank.
&ona is the modern Sone which falls into the Gahga near
Patna. Megasthencs calls it Erannaboas (Hiranyavaha) at whose

196 Brhat Samhita
junction with the Gartga was situated Pataliputa, the celebrated
Maurya capital.
Payo$n1 is identified by Cunningham with the Pahoj, a tribu-
tary of the Yamuna between the Sindh and Betwa. According to
others it is to be identified with the Paisuni or Paisundi in Bundel-
khand.
PSra or Paravati is a tributary of the Chambal rising in Bho-
pal. The old town of Padmavatj (modern Pawaya) was situated
at the confluence of the Paravati and Sindhu.
Tnpi, modern Taptl, rises in the Satpura hills and falls into
the Arabian Sea.
There are altogether 16 names of rivers coming under the
jurisdiction of Mars.
